what_it_checks: hermes binary is resolvable on PATH
failure_symptom: All tool/subprocess calls to the host fail immediately
likely_causes: Hermes not installed
PATH not exported in this shell/agent env
fix_steps: Run: which hermes
If missing, install/repair Hermes Agent and ensure its bin dir is on PATH
For agents, confirm the spawned shell inherits PATH
change_robust: Uses shutil.which at runtime — independent of any hardcoded path
detection_method: shutil.which(ctx.hermes_bin) is not None

what_it_checks: codex binary is resolvable on PATH
failure_symptom: The Codex adapter cannot start any codex exec model call
likely_causes: Codex CLI not installed
PATH not exported in this shell/agent env
fix_steps: Run: which codex
If missing, install/repair Codex CLI and ensure its bin dir is on PATH
For agents, confirm the spawned shell inherits PATH
change_robust: Uses shutil.which at runtime — independent of any hardcoded path
detection_method: shutil.which('codex') is not None

what_it_checks: Whether the Codex executable is available on PATH or at the configured CLUXION_EFFORT_ULTRACODE_CODEX_BINARY location
failure_symptom: Handler returns {ok: false, error: 'codex_not_found', hint: '..PATH..'} when using adapter='codex'
likely_causes: Codex CLI not installed or PATH does not include codex binary
CLUXION_EFFORT_ULTRACODE_CODEX_BINARY points to a nonexistent path
User's shell initialization/venv not active when plugin loads
fix_steps: Verify codex is in PATH: run 'which codex'
If env var CLUXION_EFFORT_ULTRACODE_CODEX_BINARY is set, verify path exists and is executable
Verify Codex works standalone: codex --version
change_robust: Detection probes live executable resolution and honors the configured env override.
detection_method: shutil.which(os.getenv('CLUXION_EFFORT_ULTRACODE_CODEX_BINARY', 'codex')) is not None

what_it_checks: codex --version can be launched
failure_symptom: Codex is installed but crashes before adapter calls can run
likely_causes: Broken Codex CLI install
Node wrapper cannot find the native Codex binary
Binary permissions or architecture mismatch
fix_steps: Run: codex --version
If it fails, reinstall or update Codex CLI
If using CLUXION_EFFORT_ULTRACODE_CODEX_BINARY, verify that binary directly supports --version
change_robust: Launches the live binary instead of trusting package metadata.
detection_method: `codex --version` exits 0 and prints output

what_it_checks: Whether the configured codex subprocess can be launched
failure_symptom: CodexExecutableNotFoundError or ConsensusProtocolError before any model response
likely_causes: codex binary has wrong permissions
codex wrapper has a broken shebang
Codex native binary is missing or incompatible
fix_steps: Check executable permissions: test -x $(which codex)
Run: codex --version
Reinstall Codex CLI if the wrapper cannot launch
change_robust: Detection depends on actual subprocess invocation, not cached state.
detection_method: Run configured codex binary with --version and check returncode

what_it_checks: codex exec advertises --output-last-message and --json for clean programmatic output capture
failure_symptom: Adapter would have to scrape mixed stdout and could parse hook/token noise as the final message
likely_causes: Codex CLI is too old
A wrapper named codex shadows the real CLI
Codex exec command contract changed
fix_steps: Run: codex exec --help
Upgrade Codex if --output-last-message or --json is missing
Check which codex resolves to the expected CLI
change_robust: Greps live exec --help output each run and detects CLI contract drift.
detection_method: 'codex exec', '--output-last-message', and '--json' present in `codex exec --help`

what_it_checks: hermes --version is parseable (host is a known-good build)
failure_symptom: Unknown host version; contract assumptions may not hold
likely_causes: Hermes build does not print the expected banner
Broken install
fix_steps: Run: hermes --version
Reinstall Hermes if the version banner is missing
change_robust: Parses live --version output, not a pinned constant
detection_method: regex 'Hermes Agent v<ver>' matches `hermes --version`

what_it_checks: host exposes the token-free ultracode-llm CLI bridge (plugin-registered hidden-purpose command)
failure_symptom: Standalone hermes adapter cannot launch the bridge; CLI/plugin returns hermes_bridge_unavailable
likely_causes: Plugin not loaded so ultracode-llm was never registered
Hermes host lacks register_cli_command support
An old Hermes install without the plugin bridge
fix_steps: Run: hermes ultracode-llm --help (must exit 0 with zero model/token calls)
Ensure this plugin is enabled and reloaded on the Hermes host
Reinstall/update cluxion-agentplugin-effort-ultracode if the subcommand is missing
change_robust: Probes live `hermes ultracode-llm --help` each run — token-free and detects missing bridge registration
detection_method: `hermes ultracode-llm --help` exits 0

what_it_checks: this plugin's entry point is registered under hermes_agent.plugins and import+register() works
failure_symptom: Plugin not loaded by Hermes; its tools/subcommand are absent
likely_causes: pip install incomplete
entry point metadata missing
register() import error
fix_steps: Run: python -c "import importlib.metadata as m; print([e.value for e in m.entry_points(group='hermes_agent.plugins') if 'cluxion-agentplugin-effort-ultracode' in e.name.lower()])"
Reinstall: pip install --force-reinstall cluxion-agentplugin-effort-ultracode
Confirm the entry module imports and exposes a callable register
change_robust: Reads live importlib.metadata.entry_points(), not a config file
detection_method: entry_points(group='hermes_agent.plugins') contains this dist and register is callable

what_it_checks: this plugin's toolset 'ultracode' is listed and enabled by `hermes tools list`
failure_symptom: Model cannot see this plugin's tools; --toolsets silently ignores it
likely_causes: Plugin failed to register its toolset
Hermes toolset registration API changed
fix_steps: Run: hermes tools list | grep ultracode
If missing, reinstall cluxion-agentplugin-effort-ultracode and restart the host
Verify register_tool used a valid toolset name
change_robust: Queries live `hermes tools list` for 'ultracode' — catches contract drift on Hermes upgrade
detection_method: 'ultracode' appears under plugin toolsets in `hermes tools list`

what_it_checks: installed distribution version equals the package __version__
failure_symptom: Stale or mismatched install; fixes/behaviors may not be the deployed ones
likely_causes: Partial upgrade
multiple installs shadowing each other
fix_steps: Run: pip show cluxion-agentplugin-effort-ultracode
Force a clean reinstall: pip install --force-reinstall cluxion-agentplugin-effort-ultracode
change_robust: Compares live importlib.metadata.version() to __version__ — no hardcoding
detection_method: importlib.metadata.version('cluxion-agentplugin-effort-ultracode') == __version__

what_it_checks: Whether the Hermes executable is available on PATH or at the configured CLUXION_EFFORT_ULTRACODE_HERMES_BINARY location
failure_symptom: ConsensusProtocolError with message 'Hermes executable not found: ...' and handler returns {ok: false, error: 'hermes_not_found', hint: '..PATH..'} at plugin invocation
likely_causes: Hermes not installed or PATH does not include hermes binary
CLUXION_EFFORT_ULTRACODE_HERMES_BINARY env var points to nonexistent path
User's shell initialization/venv not active when plugin loads
Hermes bootstrap incomplete (check ~/.hermes/.hermes-bootstrap-complete exists)
fix_steps: Verify hermes is in PATH: run 'which hermes' - should return /path/to/hermes
If missing, check Hermes installation: hermes --version should return version string
If env var CLUXION_EFFORT_ULTRACODE_HERMES_BINARY is set, verify path exists: test -x $CLUXION_EFFORT_ULTRACODE_HERMES_BINARY
Verify Hermes bootstrap completed: test -f ~/.hermes/.hermes-bootstrap-complete
If bootstrap incomplete, run: hermes --check-setup or similar bootstrap command
Ensure venv is active if Hermes was installed in venv (hermes --version should work outside venv for global install)
change_robust: Detection probes the live hermes binary availability (FileNotFoundError on subprocess.run), not config files. Stays valid across hermes path/version changes.
detection_method: Attempt to spawn subprocess using HermesSubprocessLlm(binary=...).complete('test'); catch FileNotFoundError which is wrapped as HermesExecutableNotFoundError

what_it_checks: Whether the configured hermes binary can be launched and does not crash immediately (permission, shebang, corrupted binary)
failure_symptom: HermesExecutableNotFoundError or hermes_bridge_unavailable during standalone adapter execution
likely_causes: hermes binary has wrong permissions (not executable)
hermes script has broken shebang or encoding issues
hermes executable is for wrong architecture/OS
shell script wrapper missing dependencies (e.g., Python path)
Hermes bootstrap corruption (binary mismatch with venv)
fix_steps: Check hermes permissions: test -x $(which hermes)
If not executable, fix: chmod +x $(which hermes)
Verify hermes runs standalone: hermes --version (should print version without errors)
Check system Python in hermes shebang: head -1 $(which hermes) should show #!/path/to/python3 or #!/usr/bin/env python3
If shebang is broken, reinstall Hermes via bootstrap
Check host macOS version: hermes may require macOS 11+ for native arm64/x86_64 binaries
If mixed arch (e.g., Intel hermes on Apple Silicon), reinstall: hermes --reinstall or check ARCHFLAGS
change_robust: Detection depends on actual subprocess invocation (OSError from subprocess.run), not cached state. Detects corruption at runtime.
detection_method: Attempt HermesSubprocessLlm().complete('test'); catch OSError (permission/exec format errors) and compare stderr/stdout from subprocess

what_it_checks: Whether hermes ultracode-llm --help is available (bridge capability; token-free)
failure_symptom: Bridge help exits non-zero or is unrecognized; standalone adapter returns hermes_bridge_unavailable
likely_causes: Plugin CLI bridge not registered on the host
Wrapper script in PATH shadows real hermes and does not expose ultracode-llm
Hermes installation incomplete or rolled back
User alias/shell function overrides hermes with custom script
fix_steps: Check which hermes resolves to: which hermes
Run: hermes ultracode-llm --help (must exit 0, no model call)
Ensure cluxion-agentplugin-effort-ultracode is installed and enabled
If a wrapper shadows hermes, point CLUXION_EFFORT_ULTRACODE_HERMES_BINARY at the real binary
change_robust: Detection runs actual hermes ultracode-llm --help and checks exit code, not static version strings.
detection_method: `hermes ultracode-llm --help` exits 0

what_it_checks: Whether CLUXION_EFFORT_ULTRACODE_HERMES_TIMEOUT env var (if set) is numeric and positive
failure_symptom: Handler returns {ok: false, error: 'invalid_timeout', message: 'CLUXION_EFFORT_ULTRACODE_HERMES_TIMEOUT must be numeric|greater than zero'} at plugin invocation
likely_causes: CLUXION_EFFORT_ULTRACODE_HERMES_TIMEOUT set to non-numeric value (e.g., '30s', 'infinity')
CLUXION_EFFORT_ULTRACODE_HERMES_TIMEOUT set to 0 or negative number
Env var contains whitespace or stray characters (e.g., '120 ') - usually ok, but exotic shells may not strip
User .env or shell profile has malformed export
fix_steps: Check current value: echo $CLUXION_EFFORT_ULTRACODE_HERMES_TIMEOUT
If unset, nothing to fix (defaults to 120 seconds)
If set, verify numeric: must be a float like 120, 300.5, 60.0
Remove invalid suffix: '30s' -> '30', 'infinity' -> '600' (large value)
Ensure positive: if value <= 0, change to reasonable value like 120 or 300
Update shell profile/env file and reload shell or use: export CLUXION_EFFORT_ULTRACODE_HERMES_TIMEOUT=120
change_robust: Detection parses env var directly and validates type/range in code. Stays valid across any env setup method.
detection_method: Call plugin._timeout_from_env(); if ValueError thrown with message containing 'numeric' or 'greater than zero', timeout config is invalid

what_it_checks: Whether the local Hermes JSON-object parser accepts a valid object without making a model call
failure_symptom: The deterministic parser contract rejects a known-valid JSON object
likely_causes: Hermes model hallucinates or adds Markdown code fences around JSON (code fence stripping fails if nested/malformed)
Hermes returns commentary/preamble before JSON object (JSON parser fails, retry instruction ignored)
Hermes returns JSON array or primitive instead of object: [1,2,3] or '"string"' instead of {...}
Hermes timeout causes truncated/invalid JSON output
The selected model does not reliably follow structured-output instructions
fix_steps: Confirm the token-free bridge exists: hermes ultracode-llm --help
Run one live adapter smoke through the public CLI: cluxion-ultracode consensus --question 'Return a small decision' --adapter hermes --agents 2 --rounds 0
If a model override is requested, configure plugins.entries.cluxion-agentplugin-effort-ultracode.llm.allow_model_override and allowed_models explicitly
Otherwise remove the override and let Hermes select its configured default model
Inspect the typed invalid_model_output message; the raw bridge protocol accepts JSON on stdin only and has no positional prompt arguments
change_robust: Detection calls the same local parser used by the Hermes adapter and remains token-free; live model compliance is outside this doctor probe.
detection_method: Call _parse_json_object on a known-valid object and verify the parsed boolean field

what_it_checks: Whether plugin.CONSENSUS_SCHEMA matches the contract that Hermes register_tool() expects: name, description, parameters with required/properties
failure_symptom: Hermes plugin loading fails with contract validation error, or tool does not appear in Hermes tool list, or tool invocation fails with 'required parameter question missing'
likely_causes: Plugin version mismatch: schema updated in plugin but Hermes contract/loader expects old schema
CONSENSUS_SCHEMA hardcoded defaults changed (e.g., 'rounds' default changed from 3 to 5)
register_tool() signature changed in host Hermes (new required parameter not provided)
Plugin not installed or reloaded after update (old .pyc or venv cache)
toolset name changed ('ultracode' vs 'consensus' vs something else)
fix_steps: Check plugin version: python3 -c 'from cluxion_effort_ultracode import __version__; print(__version__)'
Verify schema in source: grep -A 50 'CONSENSUS_SCHEMA' src/cluxion_effort_ultracode/plugin.py - should show name, description, parameters
Confirm required fields: schema['parameters']['required'] should include 'question'
Reload plugin: if in venv, reinstall: pip install -e . --force-reinstall
Check Hermes compat: hermes --list-plugins should show cluxion-agentplugin-effort-ultracode, hermes tool list should show cluxion_consensus
If register_tool contract changed, check Hermes version: hermes --version should match plugin README requirements (usually 0.14+)
change_robust: Detection inspects actual CONSENSUS_SCHEMA constant at runtime and validates structure. Detects schema drift between plugin versions and Hermes contract expectations.
detection_method: Call plugin.register(ctx) where ctx records register_tool() calls; inspect ctx.tools[0]['schema'] and verify keys: name='cluxion_consensus', description is non-empty, parameters is dict with 'required' and 'properties', 'question' is in required and properties

what_it_checks: Whether the llm_factory passed to build_consensus_handler() is callable and returns an object with a .complete() method
failure_symptom: Handler returns {ok: false, error: 'ValueError', message: 'llm_factory must be callable|must return an object with complete(...)'} at plugin invocation
likely_causes: build_consensus_handler() called with non-callable llm_factory (e.g., llm_factory=None or llm_factory='not_a_function')
llm_factory is callable but returns object without .complete() method
A standalone handler uses the Hermes bridge while a registered Hermes handler must receive the host ctx.llm surface
Hermes adapter created but .complete() method signature changed or missing
fix_steps: Verify llm_factory passed to build_consensus_handler() is a callable: type(llm_factory).__name__ should be 'function' or 'type'
Test the factory contract directly: result = llm_factory('hermes', timeout_seconds=120); hasattr(result, 'complete') should be True
If using custom LLM adapter, ensure .complete(prompt, *, schema=None) method exists with correct signature
For a registered Hermes plugin, verify ctx.llm is present and the handler factory receives it
For standalone use, verify the token-free bridge capability with hermes ultracode-llm --help
change_robust: Detection invokes llm_factory at runtime and checks .complete() existence, not static inspection. Detects missing/broken adapters at execution time.
detection_method: Call plugin.build_consensus_handler(llm_factory); invoke handler with valid args; if ValueError raised with message 'llm_factory must be callable' or 'must return an object with complete(...)', factory contract broken

what_it_checks: Whether LLM responses include all required fields for AgentPosition: stance, rationale, evidence, confidence; also checks types (stance/rationale are non-empty strings, evidence is non-empty list of strings, confidence is numeric)
failure_symptom: Handler returns {ok: false, error: 'ConsensusProtocolError', message: 'agent-N response missing required fields ...'} at plugin invocation during round 0
likely_causes: LLM model returns incomplete JSON object (missing 'stance', 'evidence', etc.)
LLM returns misspelled keys ('Stance' instead of 'stance')
evidence is empty list [] (LLM says 'no evidence')
confidence is string '0.8' instead of float 0.8
rationale or stance is empty string or whitespace-only
LLM returns non-JSON string when schema is provided (JSON parse fails but error not clear)
fix_steps: Run a live public-CLI smoke: cluxion-ultracode consensus --question 'Choose A or B with evidence' --adapter hermes --agents 2 --rounds 0
Verify field names in response: JSON keys must be exactly 'stance', 'rationale', 'evidence', 'confidence' (case-sensitive)
Check evidence is non-empty list: evidence should be array with at least one string, e.g. ['Evidence for stance']
Verify confidence is numeric: should be number like 0.75, not string '0.75'
Check rationale and stance are non-empty: must have non-whitespace text
If using custom LLM adapter, verify it returns dict matching POSITION_SCHEMA, not a string
change_robust: Detection validates actual LLM response structure at runtime via AgentPosition parsing. Detects schema drift from LLM model changes or prompt misalignment.
detection_method: Run ConsensusEngine.decide(); if ConsensusProtocolError raised with message 'response missing required fields: ...' or 'evidence must be a list of strings|include at least one non-empty item|confidence must be numeric', position validation failed

what_it_checks: Whether debate round (round >= 1) responses have valid debate structure: conceded and maintained must be lists of {point, reason} objects; if stance changes, at least one point must be conceded; any debate point must have non-empty reason
failure_symptom: Handler returns {ok: false, error: 'ConsensusProtocolError', message: 'agent-N must either concede specific points or maintain|changed stance without conceding a specific point and reason'} during debate round
likely_causes: LLM returns empty conceded[] and empty maintained[] (no debate movement)
LLM returns debate points with empty reason field: {'point': 'X', 'reason': ''}
LLM changes stance but doesn't concede any points (violates protocol)
LLM returns conceded/maintained as non-list (string, object, etc.)
LLM returns debate point items that are not objects (strings, numbers)
Round 0 response (independent positions) incorrectly includes conceded/maintained fields
fix_steps: Inspect DEBATE_SCHEMA locally and verify conceded and maintained are required arrays
Verify conceded and maintained are non-empty lists if stance changes: if stance differs from prior round, conceded must have at least one {point: '...', reason: '...'}
Check reason fields are non-empty strings: reason: '' is invalid, must be reason: 'Reason why conceding/maintaining'
If round 0 (independent round), ensure no conceded/maintained in response (only stance, rationale, evidence, confidence)
Test debate prompt: ensure LLM sees prior positions clearly and is asked to concede/maintain with reasons
If LLM avoids taking positions, use stronger prompt: 'You MUST either concede or maintain specific points, not just general agreement'
change_robust: Detection validates debate point structure and concession rules at runtime during debate round execution. Detects LLM protocol violations or prompt ambiguities.
detection_method: Run ConsensusEngine.decide() with max_rounds >= 1; if ConsensusProtocolError raised with message 'must either concede specific points or maintain|without a reason|changed stance without conceding', debate validation failed

what_it_checks: Whether debate round response is either a dict/Mapping or a valid JSON string that can be parsed into a dict; detects non-JSON strings or non-dict types
failure_symptom: Handler returns {ok: false, error: 'ConsensusProtocolError', message: 'agent-N returned non-JSON text for structured output|returned JSON that is not an object|returned unsupported response type'} during debate round
likely_causes: LLM returns plain text prose instead of JSON (e.g., 'I agree with agent-2...')
LLM returns JSON array instead of object: [{...}] or ['A', 'B']
LLM returns JSON primitive: 123, 'string', true, null
Hermes bridge JSON repair retry mechanism exhausted (first attempt non-JSON, retry also non-JSON)
LLM wraps JSON in extra commentary that is not stripped by code-fence handler
fix_steps: Run a live public-CLI smoke with one debate round: cluxion-ultracode consensus --question 'Choose A or B with evidence' --adapter hermes --agents 2 --rounds 1
Verify the token-free bridge capability separately with hermes ultracode-llm --help
If JSON is wrapped in code fences with preamble, check _strip_code_fence: should remove markdown ```json ... ``` but not plain prose
Test retry message: Hermes is given a retry instruction after first failure, should be stricter: 'Return ONLY raw JSON. No Markdown fences, no commentary, no prose.'
If the selected model keeps returning prose, choose a model that reliably follows JSON-only instructions
change_robust: Detection parses actual LLM response and validates JSON type at runtime. Detects LLM non-compliance with structured output instructions.
detection_method: Run ConsensusEngine.decide() with max_rounds >= 1; if ConsensusProtocolError raised with message 'returned non-JSON text for structured output|returned unsupported response type', response parsing failed

what_it_checks: Whether consensus engine produces valid ConsensusResult with correct status, decision, and dissent fields based on unanimity check outcome
failure_symptom: Plugin returns result with status='unanimous' but decision=None, or status='no_consensus' but dissent is empty, or result cannot be serialized to JSON (e.g., contains non-JSON-serializable objects)
likely_causes: Consensus engine has logic bug (unanimity check result doesn't match result construction)
normalize_stance() returns empty string for all stances (edge case in Unicode normalization)
Result serialization (asdict(result)) includes non-JSON types (custom objects, datetime, etc.)
ConsensusResult dataclass definition changed and fields are missing or renamed
fix_steps: Verify engine unanimity logic: if _is_unanimous(positions) returns True, then consensus.status must be 'unanimous' and decision must equal positions[0].stance
Test normalize_stance: python3 -c 'from cluxion_effort_ultracode.core.consensus import normalize_stance; print(normalize_stance("test"))'
Check ConsensusResult fields: python3 -c 'from cluxion_effort_ultracode.core.types import ConsensusResult; print(ConsensusResult.__dataclass_fields__.keys())'
Test JSON serialization: python3 -c 'from dataclasses import asdict; import json; json.dumps(asdict(result))' should not raise TypeError
Verify AgentPosition, DebatePoint, Dissent all have JSON-serializable fields (no custom objects)
change_robust: Detection validates result invariants at runtime after engine execution. Detects logic bugs or dataclass evolution issues.
detection_method: Run ConsensusEngine.decide(); inspect returned ConsensusResult. If status='unanimous', decision should be non-empty string and dissent should be empty list. If status='no_consensus', decision should be None and dissent should be non-empty. If either invariant violated, result is malformed

what_it_checks: Whether debate rounds might loop indefinitely or exceed reasonable cost bounds; specifically checks that consensus engine terminates within max_rounds and counts LLM calls (worst case: agents * (rounds + 1) calls)
failure_symptom: Plugin execution takes unexpectedly long, or Hermes/provider usage accounting shows many costly model calls. No hard failure, but degraded performance.
likely_causes: max_rounds set to very large value (e.g., 10 or 100) by caller
agents_count set very high (e.g., 10 agents)
LLM keeps producing marginally different stances that don't normalize to unanimous (but also don't fail validation)
Debate continues without any agent conceding (all agents maintain all points forever)
No built-in cost limit (only termination on max_rounds or unanimity)
fix_steps: Check default params: agents=3, max_rounds=3 means worst case 12 calls. Verify caller doesn't override to higher values.
Monitor actual rounds: ConsensusResult.rounds should be <= max_rounds. If equal, debate ran full length without unanimity.
If high cost observed, lower max_rounds in handler call: plugin.build_consensus_handler() -> handler({..., rounds=1})
If agents > 3, reduce: plugin.build_consensus_handler() -> handler({..., agents=2})
Check if LLM is in a loop (same stances every round): log transcript to see if positions are changing. If static, unanimity should be reached or max_rounds limit should trigger.
Add runtime cost limit if needed (future enhancement): abort if total_calls > threshold
change_robust: Detection counts actual rounds in ConsensusResult.transcript and validates against max_rounds. Stays valid across future changes to debate logic.
detection_method: Track len(ConsensusResult.transcript) and agent count. Cost = agents * (max_rounds + 1). If actual rounds exceed max_rounds, or if LLM call count would be > 12 for default settings, log cost warning. Verify with manual round count.

what_it_checks: Whether plugin.register(ctx) can be called with a host context that may or may not have register_tool method; ensures graceful degradation if host is not Hermes-like
failure_symptom: Plugin loading fails with AttributeError or TypeError when host context doesn't have register_tool or when it's not callable
likely_causes: Host context doesn't implement register_tool method (not Hermes-like)
register_tool exists but is not a callable (e.g., attribute is a string or property)
Plugin assumes all hosts support register_tool (not defensive)
fix_steps: Check host context interface: if using non-Hermes host, ensure it either has register_tool(name, toolset, schema, handler, ...) method or plugin.register() is never called
If host has register_tool, verify it's callable: callable(ctx.register_tool) should be True
If host doesn't support plugins, plugin.register(ctx) should gracefully return (no error, tool just not registered)
Test defensive registration: python3 -c 'from cluxion_effort_ultracode.plugin import register; register(object())' should not raise exception
change_robust: Detection tests actual plugin.register() behavior with various host contexts. Ensures portable plugin code.
detection_method: Call plugin.register(ctx) where ctx is missing register_tool method. Should not raise exception. Then call register(ctx) where ctx.register_tool is non-callable. Should not raise exception.

what_it_checks: Whether the standalone Hermes adapter exposes its bridge runner used to classify process return codes
failure_symptom: The adapter runner is missing, so nonzero bridge results cannot be classified
likely_causes: Hermes LLM call failed (e.g., API key invalid, model unavailable, network error)
Hermes subprocess crashed (segfault, panic, Python exception)
The registered ultracode-llm bridge command is missing or incompatible
A requested model override was denied or is not in the host allowlist
Hermes configuration is broken (config.yaml missing or malformed)
fix_steps: Verify the token-free command surface: hermes ultracode-llm --help
Run the supported public CLI path: cluxion-ultracode consensus --question 'Bridge smoke' --adapter hermes --agents 2 --rounds 0
Inspect Hermes host logs for the underlying bridge failure; raw stderr is intentionally not copied into user-facing errors
Verify Hermes config and provider credentials using the host's documented diagnostics
If using --models, verify plugins.entries.cluxion-agentplugin-effort-ultracode.llm.allow_model_override and allowed_models
Increase timeout if Hermes is slow: CLUXION_EFFORT_ULTRACODE_HERMES_TIMEOUT=300 (5 minutes)
change_robust: Detection introspects the adapter runner without launching Hermes or spending tokens; runtime returncode paths are covered by adapter tests.
detection_method: Verify HermesSubprocessLlm._run_bridge_once is callable

what_it_checks: Whether the standalone adapter accepts and retains a positive timeout configuration
failure_symptom: HermesSubprocessLlm(timeout_seconds=1) does not retain the validated value
likely_causes: Hermes model is slow (large prompt, complex reasoning, overloaded API)
Network latency high (API call takes time)
CLUXION_EFFORT_ULTRACODE_HERMES_TIMEOUT too short for prompt complexity (default 120s may be tight)
System under heavy load (hermes process starved of CPU)
Hermes API backend is degraded or offline
fix_steps: Increase timeout: export CLUXION_EFFORT_ULTRACODE_HERMES_TIMEOUT=300 (or higher)
Measure the supported public path: time cluxion-ultracode consensus --question 'Latency smoke' --adapter hermes --agents 2 --rounds 0
Check system load: top or similar - if CPU/memory maxed, hermes may be slow
Simplify prompts: if debate prompts are very long, reduce context or evidence length
Check the configured provider's service status with its official status page or Hermes diagnostics
Profile consensus loop: add logging to see which agent/round is slowest, may help identify bottleneck
change_robust: Detection constructs the adapter only and is token-free; runtime deadline enforcement is covered by adapter tests.
detection_method: Construct HermesSubprocessLlm(timeout_seconds=1) and verify timeout_seconds == 1

what_it_checks: Whether LLM adapter's .complete() method has correct signature: complete(prompt: str, *, schema: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, model: str | None = None) -> Mapping[str, Any] | str
failure_symptom: TypeError or AttributeError during engine._initial_positions() or engine._debate_round() call, indicating complete() signature mismatch or LLM object missing complete method
likely_causes: Custom LLM adapter complete() method doesn't accept **kwargs (llm.complete() called with schema/model kwargs, but method doesn't accept them)
complete() has wrong parameter name (e.g., 'prompt_text' instead of 'prompt')
complete() is not a method but an attribute (e.g., lambda function assigned to class instance)
LLM object is None or not an object with .complete() method
fix_steps: Verify LLM class has complete() method: inspect(llm.__class__).methods or hasattr(llm, 'complete')
Check method signature: inspect.signature(llm.complete) should show (prompt, *, schema=None) or compatible
Test calling complete directly: llm.complete('test', schema=None) should work without error
If custom adapter, ensure method signature matches LlmPort protocol: complete(self, prompt: str, *, schema: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, model: str | None = None) -> Mapping[str, Any] | str
If using HermesSubprocessLlm, verify it's instantiated correctly: HermesSubprocessLlm() or HermesSubprocessLlm(binary='hermes', timeout_seconds=120, model=None)
change_robust: Detection runs engine with actual LLM and validates method calls work. Detects signature mismatches and protocol violations at runtime.
detection_method: Call ConsensusEngine(llm, ...).decide(...) where llm.complete() has wrong signature. If TypeError or AttributeError raised during call, signature mismatch detected.
